install.title: Installation Guide
install.subtitle: Choose the installation method for your operating system. Setup takes just a few minutes.

windows.name: Windows
windows.desc: Choose the installer that's right for you
windows.steps: Download .exe installer → Double-click to run wizard → Choose installation path (optional) → Complete installation and launch

macos.name: macOS
macos.desc: Choose the installer that's right for you
macos.steps: Download .dmg file → Double-click and drag to Applications → Allow in System Settings on first launch → Enjoy native macOS experience

linux.name: Linux
linux.desc: Choose the installer that's right for you
linux.steps: Download platform-specific package → Debian: sudo dpkg -i jadekit_*.deb → AppImage: chmod +x jadekit-*.AppImage → Launch from application menu

systemRequirements.title: System Requirements
systemRequirements.networkValues: Internet connection required (local proxy can alleviate restrictions)

q1.question: Is JadeKit secure?
q1.answer: Yes. All API tokens are encrypted and stored locally, only used for communication with AI providers. Nothing is uploaded to third-party servers. The code is fully open source for audit.

q2.question: Does it require internet?
q2.answer: Basic configuration features work offline, but token switching, fetching available models, and syncing MCP servers require network connection. The built-in local proxy helps with network restrictions.

q3.question: Which AI providers are supported?
q3.answer: Supports Anthropic official API, Azure OpenAI, and any third-party API compatible with OpenAI format. Custom proxy configuration allows easy switching between providers.

q4.question: How to uninstall?
q4.answer: Windows: Uninstall via "Settings > Apps"; macOS: Delete app from Applications and clean up ~/.jadekit; Linux: Use package manager or delete AppImage.
